Minh: Is "--ofed-version=inkernel" a currently working option?  I don't see anywhere where "inkernel" is accepted as a valid option.

If that use-case is desired, I would not be against it but it probably does not belong in this ticket.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

(8 comments)

I think the only real issue among all of my comments is the implication that we might have mis-detected the OFED devel tree in the error message given to the user when they provide an incorrect path.  We should never mis-detect it and so should not imply to the user that we might have.  If you think there is a chance we might have, we ought to test and report that condition separately as a bug in the detection.

Many of the other things are worth fixing though IMHO, including better/more verbose error messages since they seem a bit cryptic as they are but along with everything else (aside from the above implication) are optional though.  I would not -1 for any of them.

SLES builds fail due to patch-set #9 ...

Patch Set 9:

Humm, in fact I was wrong in my previous comment, because the Module.symvers replacement of in-Kernel IB driver symbols should only occur if an OFED version is installed and we want to build against it.
Patch-set #10 should fix this.

Patch Set 15:

Humm, doing more testing to better track other possible regressions, I found that the test to add "patches" directory check (to address recent OFED versions that no longer use "kernel_patches" dir name) collides with inkernel-IB build, so I need to only apply it when looking for OFED headers.

(6 comments)

The tabs/indenting are all just suggestions that I wouldn't even typically make but this is such a complicated piece of code with so many branches and mixed m4 and bash syntax that I think getting the tabs lined up correctly would add loads of readability to the code.

My biggest concern is the s/,/|/g in the help text but I won't -1 for that.  I would strongly like to see it changed in any subsequent patch if you decide not to make a new patch just to fix it.

parents.subject: LU-6218 osd-zfs: increase redundancy for meta data

commit.subject: LU-5953 build: use installed OFED by default
commit.message:
LU-5953 build: use installed OFED by default

During LNET autoconf phase, if OFED installed and its devel headers
are available, default to use it instead of in-Kernel IB driver.

Also handle wrong case where OFED installed but not devel preventing
to build against OFED. Had to add new "patches" vs "kernel_patches"
dir name use in recent OFED versions and to avoid its check to
collide with inkernel-IB builds case.

Current OFED headers detection mechanism allow for non-standard
prefix but relies on "ofed_info" command and on "%prefix/openib"
link (both are ok for 1.5.x and 3.x versions), and should work
for both source and DKMS Lustre builds.

Test-Parameters: nettypes=o2ib
Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
